title: Agent Learning Approaches: A Practitioner's Taxonomy and Empirical Comparison
description:
Agent Learning Approaches is a systematic empirical comparison of seven agent learning strategies (CLL, RAG, Reflexion, ICL, CLL+RAG, Debate, SimLLM) across 14 reproducible tasks. Includes a taxonomy of 20 agent learning paradigms organised into 5 categories (Weight-Based, Context-Based, Hybrid, Structural, Meta) and a 9×7 trade-off matrix as a decision framework for practitioners.

Key findings: CLL (file-based institutional memory) wins 6 of 8 core tasks but fails on complex coding-agent scenarios where RAG dominates (50+ rules, code review, error triage). Reflexion wins on pattern shifts. Debate is the most robust allrounder. McNemar χ² tests confirm CLL vs RAG differences are statistically significant (p < 0.0001), Cohen's h effect sizes quantify the differences. Multi-seed analysis confirms σ=0.000 on 4/7 tasks.

Reproducibility: 181 deterministic tests, no API key required for full reproduction (SimLLM mode). Real-LLM validation with Claude Haiku confirmed strategy rankings using 150 API calls (~$0.04).

Open source under Apache 2.0.
notes: Single-author research artifact. 181 deterministic unit tests. SimLLM mode requires no API key for full reproducibility. Real-LLM validation via Claude Haiku.
